requirements.evidence: ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButtonStyle.swift baseTokens(for:in:) maps `.primary` to `theme.colors.primary.default` + `theme.colors.onPrimary.default`; `cornerRadius(for:in:)` returns `theme.radius.md`; `typography(for:in:)` returns `theme.type.label.md`. Covered by LSButtonTests.test_primary_variant_resolves_action_primary_tokens.

ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButtonStyle.swift pressedTokens(for:in:) maps `.primary` to `theme.colors.primary.pressed ?? theme.colors.accent.pressed ?? theme.colors.primary.default`. Covered by LSButtonTests.test_primary_pressed_state_resolves_pressed_token.

Disabled interaction is suppressed by `.disabled(isDisabled)` in ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift and by `LSButton.dispatch`, and disabled colors are resolved in LSButtonStyle.disabledTokens(...). But the style never applies a disabled opacity token: `filled`, `bordered`, and `unbordered` hard-code `opacity: 1`, and the outline disabled branch also returns `opacity: 1`.
requirements.remediation: Set disabled resolved tokens to use the required opacity token and add a test that asserts the disabled opacity path, not just colors/callback suppression.

requirements.evidence: ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift routes the icon slot through `LSButtonIcon`, which now renders `LSIcon(name:size:resolvedColorOverride:)`; the previous custom Canvas fallback path is gone. Outline tokens still resolve border/background/foreground and spacing/icon-size helpers. Covered by LSButtonTests.test_outline_variant_with_leading_icon_renders_chip_layout and test_button_icon_slot_routes_through_lsicon_without_hidden_or_canvas_fallbacks.

ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/Stories/LSButtonStories.swift registers all six `atoms.button.{variant}` stories with tier `.atom` and argTypes for `title`, `isDisabled`, and `leadingIcon`; ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/LaneShadowStories.swift aggregates `LSButtonStories.all`.

ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButtonStyle.swift metrics(for:in:) returns `minWidth: theme.touchTarget.minTouchTarget` and `minHeight: theme.control.minHeight`; current theme tokens are 44pt. Covered by LSButtonTests.test_minimum_touch_target_44pt_on_smallest_size.

ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift funnels actions through `LSButton.dispatch(isDisabled:action:)`, which invokes the callback exactly once when enabled. Covered by LSButtonTests.test_action_fires_exactly_once_per_press.
ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift contains no literal `Color(...)` or hard-coded color usage; the prompt also reports the grep gate passed.
ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift contains no `Image(systemName:)`; the icon slot now goes through `LSIcon`, and the prompt reports the grep gate passed.

The task outcome and concept require default/hover/pressed/disabled/focus. ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButtonStyle.swift defines no hover state at all, `resolvedState(isPressed:)` can never produce one, and the focused branch uses a single `theme.colors.ring.default` 1pt stroke for every variant instead of the required 3px variant-colored focus ring.
requirements.remediation: Add hover handling for Catalyst, implement the specified focus-ring treatment per variant, and add tests/stories that exercise hover and focus states.

findings.evidence: `LSButtonInteractionState` has only `default/pressed/disabled/focused`, `resolvedState(isPressed:)` never emits hover, and the focused branch applies `theme.colors.ring.default` as a thin border for every variant. The spec requires a hover state plus a 3px offset focus ring using the variant's own color.
findings.fix: Model hover explicitly, wire it into the style on Catalyst, and replace the current generic focus border with the required variant-specific focus ring effect and coverage.

findings.evidence: The style applies `.opacity(tokens.opacity)`, but every shared token constructor returns `opacity: 1`, and the outline disabled branch also returns `opacity: 1`. Disabled buttons therefore do not consume the disabled opacity token required by the task/concept.
findings.fix: Propagate the disabled opacity token through resolved tokens for every variant and add an XCTest that asserts the disabled opacity path.

findings.evidence: `LSIcon.init(name:size:color:resolvedColorOverride:)` is public and accepts an arbitrary `Color?`, which allows any caller to bypass the semantic `IconContentColor` contract. That is broader than the button-specific remediation needs.
findings.fix: Narrow the override to internal/fileprivate scope or provide a narrowly scoped internal helper so only LSButton can pass a resolved foreground color.

summary: The icon regression is fixed: LSButton now routes through LSIcon and the custom fallback Canvas path is removed. This still is not approvable because the button state matrix remains incomplete and incorrect for hover/focus, disabled opacity is not token-driven, and the LSIcon API widening is broader than necessary.
